name: AMI Labs
summary: Yann LeCun's AI startup building 'world models' - AI that learns from reality, not just language. Raised $1.03B seed at $3.5B pre-money valuation.

name: Legora
summary: AI platform for lawyers, now used by 800 law firms. Valuation tripled from $1.8B to $5.55B in one year. Grew from 40 to 400 employees.

name: Translucent
summary: AI-powered healthcare finance platform. $27M Series A led by GV (Google Ventures).

name: Nscale
summary: Largest European AI infrastructure funding ever. $2B Series C at $14.6B valuation. Major Microsoft partnership for 200K GPUs.

name: Quince
summary: AI-powered retail platform hitting $1B revenue. $500M Series E at $10.1B valuation.

name: Sage
summary: AI-powered senior care platform. $65M Series C led by Goldman Sachs Growth Equity.

name: Sandbar
summary: AI note-taking ring startup. Former Meta employees Mina Fahmi and Kirak Hong built Stream ring - voice note-taking wearable with privacy-focused design. Preorders sold out, users averaging 50+ interactions/day.

name: Mind Robotics
summary: Rivian spin-out building AI-powered industrial robots for factory floor deployment at scale. $500M Series A at $2B valuation. Total funding $615M since founding November 2025.

name: Wonderful
summary: Enterprise AI agent platform founded in 2025 by Bar Winkler (CEO) and Roey Lalazar (CTO). $150M Series B at $2B valuation. 350 employees scaling to 900. Traction: 70% enterprise expansion within 3 months, 60% reduction in handling times, 80%+ containment rates.

name: Gestala
summary: Chinese BCI startup developing non-invasive ultrasound-based brain-computer interfaces. $21.6M raised just 2 months after launch at $100M-$200M valuation. First ultrasound BCI company in China. Lead program: chronic pain management. Also researching depression, PTSD, autism, stroke rehab.

name: RunSybil
summary: AI cybersecurity startup using AI agents to automatically hack company software to find security weaknesses. Co-founded by Ari Herbert-Voss (OpenAI's first security hire) and Vlad Ionescu (former Meta red team lead). AI agent Sybil conducts continuous autonomous penetration tests against live applications. Works with Cursor, Turbopuffer, Notion, Baseten, Thinking Machines Lab, plus major financial institutions and Fortune 500 companies.

name: Andromeda AI
summary: Startup helping companies rent AI infrastructure (GPUs) on-demand without long-term contracts. Platform aggregates unused GPUs from data centers of internet providers, crypto miners, etc. 100+ provider network. Annualized revenue grew from $50M (2024) to $100M (2025). Profitable since launch. Processed 1,000+ GPU transactions since launching ~2 years ago. Customers include notable AI startups and labs spending $250M-$500M/year on infrastructure.

name: Edra
summary: NYC-based automation platform that turns existing operational data (emails, logs, support tickets, chat histories) into a living knowledge base. Founded by two former Palantir engineers - Eugen Alpeza (built out major commercial accounts, launched Palantir's AI platform) and Yannis Karamanlakis (first forward deployed AI engineer at Palantir). Building 'Living Playbooks' that learn from real workflows. Customers include HubSpot, ASOS, and Cushman & Wakefield. Total funding: $30M Series A.

name: Parallel
summary: Paris-based startup developing AI agents for hospitals. Founded in 2024 by Paul Lafforgue (CEO) and co-founders. AI agents operate directly within existing hospital software as an AI layer that learns to use software like a human - enabling deployment in weeks. Initial focus on medical coding (translating clinical data into standardized codes for reimbursement). Already deployed across multiple public and private hospitals in Europe. Previously raised $3.5M seed in April 2025. Total funding: $20M Series A.

name: BusRight
summary: All-in-one student transportation technology platform. Uses AI to ensure school buses get students safely to class on time. Platform brings driver navigation, routing, student ridership visibility, parent communication, and live GPS tracking into one unified platform. 100%+ revenue growth for 5 consecutive years, serving 36 states. Reduced call volume by 80%+ in some districts.

name: Sequen
summary: AI startup bringing TikTok-style personalization to enterprise. Large Event Models (LEMs) learn from live user actions within a session - not static profiles or third-party cookies. Privacy-forward. Sub-20-millisecond latency. Processed 10 billion monthly requests in under 18 months. 5 Fortune 500 customers. 7% revenue lift for furniture company (vs 0.4% typical), 20% net revenue lift for Fetch Rewards in 11 days.

name: XBOW
summary: Seattle-based startup building an AI-driven platform that continuously emulates attackers at machine speed to uncover deep vulnerabilities. Platform probes software releases and environments to find security weaknesses that manual pen-testing might miss. The $120M Series C values the company at over $1 billion, making it one of the few cybersecurity unicorns to emerge in 2026.

name: Halcyon
summary: AI platform that ingests and normalizes U.S. energy regulatory data from all 50 states, every ISO/RTO grid operator, and FERC dockets. Enables utilities, data center operators, and developers to query and monitor energy information in natural language. In the U.S. alone, up to $850 billion will be invested in power and digital infrastructure in 2026, creating massive demand for energy project intelligence.

name: Cloaked
summary: Consumer-first privacy and security platform offering bundled solutions including identity creation, data removal, identity theft insurance, VPN, dark web monitoring, and AI-powered call screening. Founded by brothers Arjun and Abhijay Bhatnagar in 2020. Saw 10x growth last year with 350,000+ paying customers. Has protected 10 million identities and cleaned up 1 billion+ records from data broker sites. Now expanding to enterprise.

name: Standard Template Labs (STLabs)
summary: AI-first service management platform that resolves IT requests end-to-end, not just tracks them. Founded by Amit Agarwal (ex-President/CPO of Datadog). Platform uses AI to interpret requests, orchestrate workflows, and resolve them in context using a continuously updated digital twin of the enterprise. First company incubated by ICONIQ. Seed round co-led by ICONIQ and CRV.

name: Gimlet Labs
summary: Created first 'multi-silicon inference cloud' - software allowing AI workloads to run across diverse hardware simultaneously. Claims 3x-10x inference speedup for same cost. Partners with NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM, Cerebras, d-Matrix. Eight-figure revenues out of October 2025 launch; customer base doubled in 4 months. Apps currently using hardware only 15-30% of the time. Total funding: $92M.

name: Advanced Navigation
summary: Global leader in navigation and autonomous systems. Builds resilient navigation systems that work beyond GPS for sea, land, air, space. AdNav Intelligence fusion engine. 100,000+ systems deployed globally; 80%+ revenue from US and Europe; triple-digit growth. Clients include Anduril, NOAA, Hanwha, BHP, Rheinmetall, Intuitive Machines. Founded 2012 from University of Western Australia by Chris Shaw and Xavier Orr.

name: RoboForce
summary: Full-stack Physical AI robotics company deploying TITAN platform for demanding industrial jobs (solar, data centers, shipping, mining, manufacturing). Addresses critical labor shortages - 53 gigawatts of US solar delayed in 2024. 11,000+ robot orders via LOI. Spotlighted by Jensen Huang at NVIDIA GTC 2025. TITAN robot with 1mm accuracy. Team from Carnegie Mellon, Amazon Robotics, Google, Waymo, Cruise, Tesla Robotics, ABB, Apple. Deep NVIDIA collaboration.

name: PointOne
summary: AI-powered timekeeping platform for law firms. Passively tracks lawyer's computer activity to auto-complete timesheets. Grew revenue 10x since July 2025. Signed dozens of law firm customers from global 1,200-lawyer firms to solo practitioners. Total funding: $20M.

name: Interloom
summary: Munich startup solving AI 'tacit knowledge' problem for enterprises. Building 'context graph' - a living map of how problems actually get resolved within organizations. Reduced documented vs. actual operational knowledge gap from 50% to 5% at Commerzbank. Won Zurich Insurance company-wide AI competition (beating 2,000 AI-native startups).

name: Kandou AI
summary: Swiss chip interconnect startup developing copper-based alternatives to fiber optics for AI clusters. Key product Glasswing links multi-die processor components using proprietary CNRZ-5 Chord technology. Valued at $400M. Part of wave of interconnect startups raising in 2026.

name: Harvey
summary: Legal AI company offering AI tools for legal and professional services - contract analysis, compliance, due diligence, and litigation. $190M ARR (January 2026), up from $100M in August 2025. 100,000+ lawyers across 1,300 organizations. Clients include global law firms, NBCUniversal, and HSBC. Valuation $11B — one of the few AI unicorns of 2026. Total funding raised $1B+. Co-founders Winston Weinberg (CEO) and Gabe Pereyra. Plans to expand AI agents and embedded legal engineering teams globally.

name: Adonis
summary: AI orchestration platform for healthcare revenue cycle management - automates denial prevention, claim resolution, and revenue recovery for hospitals, health systems, and physician groups. Founded by brothers Akash Magoon and Aman Magoon in 2022. $40M Series C led by Quadrille Capital. Total funding: $95M. Growing demand as health systems fight AI-powered insurer denials.

name: Origin
summary: AI-powered benefits management platform. Ingest and structure fragmented benefits data across policies, contracts, renewals, vendor platforms in dozens of languages. One client (CFO estimated $750M benefits spend) expects to save $75M. Another consolidated 13 local insurance policies into one regional plan achieving 20% cost savings. Anchor customers: Pfizer, Comcast, BP. Founded by Chris Bruce and Pete Craghill (previously founded Darwin, acquired by Mercer in 2016). Value-based pricing model.

name: Cents
sector: Enterprise Software / Vertical SaaS / Laundry Industry
summary: All-in-one platform for laundromat management with point-of-sale, on-machine payments, marketing automation, AI customer service, pickup & delivery services, and live GPS tracking. 4,500+ laundromat locations processing $1B in payments annually. Largest single software investment in the laundry vertical. Serves laundromats, dry cleaners, and multi-family operations.

name: Shield AI
summary: Defense AI startup building autonomous drone systems. Nova drone became first AI-powered drone deployed in US military history. Raised $2B Series G at $12.7B valuation (up 140%). Selected by US Air Force as mission autonomy provider for CCA. Pilots 26 classes of vehicles including F-16s, jet-powered UAVs, helicopters, drone boats, and ground vehicles. Acquired Aechelon Technology (flight simulation). Gary Steele appointed as new CEO Dec 2025.

name: Granola
summary: AI meeting notes startup expanding from prosumer to enterprise. Co-founders Chris Pedregal (previously founded Socratic, acquired by Google) and Sam Stephenson. Total funding $192M. Customers include Vanta, Gusto, Thumbtack, Asana, Cursor, Lovable, Decagon, Mistral AI. Launched Spaces (team workspaces) and enterprise APIs. Valuation jumped 6x from $250M to $1.5B in under a year.

name: Lace (Lace Lithography)
summary: Norwegian startup developing helium atom beam lithography for next-gen chip manufacturing. Uses 0.1nm beam width (135x narrower than ASML's EUV light) to enable atomic-scale chip features. Could extend Moore's Law beyond current limitations. Founded July 2023 by physicist Bodil Holst and former PhD student Adria Salvador Palau. Raised $40M Series A led by Atomico with Microsoft M12 participation.

name: Blossom Health
summary: AI copilot for psychiatry. Platform augments clinicians' clinical decisions and automates back office tasks. Turns episodic care into continuous AI-driven relationship. John Zhao (CEO, ex-Athelas, ex-EverQuote) leading. Hundreds of clinicians treating 10,000+ patients across multiple states. In-network with major insurers, copays ~$22.

name: Arinna
summary: Building ultrathin solar panels from transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) for spacecraft. 32% more efficient, flexible, cheaper, more durable than legacy space solar. Lasts 15 years on orbit. Founded by Stanford PhDs Koosha Nazif (CEO) and Alex Shearer (CTO). Testing on orbit before year-end, mass production planned 2028.

name: ORAN Development (ODC)
summary: AI-Native Radio Access Network (AI-RAN) pioneer transforming cell towers into distributed compute hubs. $45M Series A from Nvidia, Booz Allen, Cisco, Nokia, AT&T, MTN, Telecom Italia. Architect of U.S.-based Odyssey platform. Turning 5G networks into AI compute fabric for Agentic AI and Physical AI applications.

name: Deccan AI
sector: AI / GenAI Data / Foundation Model Infrastructure
summary: GenAI data company running post-training and production layer for AI labs and enterprises. Runs expert human trajectories, rigorous evals, and RL environments. Works with top frontier labs and majority of Magnificent 7 companies. Global pool of 1M+ domain experts. Operational backbone in Hyderabad. Mission: 'super accurate AI' for high-stakes business logic.

name: Glimpse
sector: AI / CPG & Retail Operations / Dispute-Tracking Fintech
summary: AI platform for CPG/retail that automates financial deduction processes and dispute tracking. Helps brands recover invalid deductions from retailers. AI agents log into retailer portals, centralize documents, classify deductions, validate against supply chain/promotion data, and automatically file disputes. Works with 200+ brands including Suave and Chapstick. System gets smarter with each deduction processed (compounding data advantage). Total funding: $52M.

name: Doctronic
sector: Healthcare AI / Digital Health / AI Doctor
summary: AI doctor platform using a multi-agent system for 24/7 autonomous triage through treatment. Chatbot consults patients then transfers to licensed physicians via telehealth ($39 fee). 15M+ AI consultations, 1M+ patients globally. Study: 81% diagnosis agreement with physicians, 99.2% treatment plan agreement, zero hallucinations. Founded 2023 by Matt Pavelle (ex-CTO Moda Operandi) and Dr. Adam Oskowitz (UCSF vascular surgeon). Expanding into academic medical centers, payers, and digital health platforms.

name: Lucid Bots
summary: Full-stack robotics company manufacturing Sherpa drones (window cleaning) and Lavo robot in the US. Charlotte-based. Approaching 1,000 units sold (grew from 100 units in 5 years). Expanding from window cleaning to painting, waterproofing, and sealing. 50+ inbound leads/month for painting before marketing. Founded 2018 by Andrew Ashur (economics/liberal arts grad) who got the idea watching window washers in danger. Anti-hype approach — selling real job site performance, not demos.

name: Swish
summary: Bengaluru-based full-stack 10-minute food delivery startup. Owns kitchens, supply chain, and delivery network — dense hyperlocal clusters within 1km radius. ~20,000 orders/day across 10 micro-markets in Bengaluru, up from 5,000 four months ago. Older kitchen clusters already profitable. Cofounders: Aniket Shah (CEO), Ujjwal Sukheja, Saran S. $38M Series B led by Hara Global and Bain Capital Ventures. Valued at $139M post-money (more than 2x valuation from a year ago). Total funding: $54M.

name: Doss
summary: AI-native inventory management layer that integrates with existing accounting systems and ERPs (Rillet, Campfire, QuickBooks). Solves the problem of keeping physical goods data synced with accounting ledgers. Targets mid-market consumer brands generating $20M-$250M revenue. Cofounder/CEO Wiley Jones pivoted from core accounting product to partner with AI-native ERP companies. Competitors: NetSuite, Didero. $55M Series B co-led by Madrona and Premji Invest, with Intuit Ventures, Theory Ventures, General Catalyst participating.
